title: Gallery
wikitext:
[]: Gallery             Quote Post by Wayne » Tue Oct 18, 2005 12:40 am I was flipping through the gallery on this site, but what is this gallery from or what does it mean: "ikari_shinji_ikusei_keikaku?" Is it just fan art but yet is all in the same style?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 18, 2005 12:59 am The Shinji Ikari Raising Project - one of the NGE spin-off computer games

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Tue Oct 18, 2005 5:57 am Yeah, the Shinji version of Rei raising game! I wonder if anyone would play that? (Except for the fangirls..)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 18, 2005 8:42 am I would play a Nagisa Kaworu Ikusei Keikaku.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Oct 18, 2005 9:52 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I would play a Nagisa Kaworu Ikusei Keikaku.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Tue Oct 18, 2005 10:39 am I've been playing Ikari Shinji Ikusei Keikau and I don't like Kaworu X Shinji! I'm trying to get him to hook up with Asuka, but everythings in Kanji and I don't know what they're saying.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 7:19 pm LmaO You're playing even though everything's in Kanji. Maybe someone could patch the game, and translate the whole thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 7:40 pm superdoughboy4 wrote: LmaO You're playing even though everything's in Kanji. Maybe someone could patch the game, and translate the whole thing?

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 7:44 pm I have nothing to give, but reasonable arguments. The fun in playing a translated game, is actually understanding it, without randomly choosing options, trying to figure out what option does what.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 7:51 pm Half the fun is trying to understand the game and the fun things that happen because you dont understand

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 8:08 pm LoL That's like me playing Neon Genesis Evangelion for the N64, ROM style!!! There's an option call The Third Impact, and I was like, "Hmm... What does this do?" Then I took the mistake of choosing that option and accepting it, and it erased the whole game. LoL I didn't see any changes because I didn't beat the whole game the first time I did it, but the next time I did it, I noticed the 1 major change: Disappearing options!!! Then I came to figure that The Third Impact, was an actual Third Impact on the game's files. Heh.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Fri Oct 21, 2005 2:54 pm I love playing games in Japanese. Its so fun, trying to get what the hell is going on, and its even funnier when they do something really farfetched. Yah, like I got something called Shito Ikusei, for Wonderswan. You're pretty much Kaji trying to raise an angel. I had no idea what the hell I was doing, and my angel died because I DIDN'T LIKE, FEED IT OR SOMETHING. Eva64 is difficult on emu though because I cant read my synch ratio and depending on how high or low it is, I can execute specific attacks. Blegh.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Fri Oct 21, 2005 6:38 pm Here's a suggestion: Use Jabo's plug-in, or if you have a good enough GFX card, use Rice's. These can be found at Emu Talk. Google it because I forgot the URL. When I first played Evangelion 64, without watching the series, I had that WtF face on like this; o.0; Then after finally watching the series and being hyped to playing the game, I finally understood what the hell was going on, and finally got around to beating a few of the Angels to level 5... [Level 5 is the Asuka/Shinji synchronization stage... It's so hard... Especially with a crap graphics card, that blinks multiple C-Button commands to screw you over. Basically, it's like a DDR stage mission.]
